etymology.description: 舅(舅) (jiù, "mother's brother"); 舅(舅) (jiù, "mother's brother")
etymology.interpretation: The word 舅舅 (jiùjiu) is a reduplication of 舅(舅) (jiù, "mother's brother"). In Chinese, reduplication can emphasize a familiar or affectionate tone, especially when referring to family members. This term specifically denotes one's maternal uncle.

examples.chinese: 我舅舅住在北京(我舅舅住在北京)。
每逢暑假(每逢暑假)，我舅舅就会去旅行(我舅舅就會去旅行)。
examples.pinyin: Měiféng shǔjià, Wǒ jiùjiu jiù huì qù lǚxíng.
examples.english: Every summer vacation, my uncle goes traveling.
